records.ety: [OE. sobben; akin to AS. sefian, sifian, to complain, bewail, sefung, sifung, sobbing, lamentation; cf. OHG. sūftōn, sūftjōn, to sigh, MHG. siuften, siufzen, G. seufzen, MHG. sūft a sigh, properly, a drawing in of breath, from sūfen to drink, OHG. sūfan. Cf. Sup.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sobbed (sbd); p. pr. & vb. n. Sobbing.]

defs: To sigh with a sudden heaving of the breast, or with a kind of convulsive motion; to sigh with tears, and with a convulsive drawing in of the breath.
quotes: Sobbing is the same thing [as sighing], stronger.
She sighed, she sobbed, and, furious with despair. She rent her garments, and she tore her hair.

defs: The act of sobbing; a convulsive sigh, or inspiration of the breath, as in sorrow.
quotes: Break, heart, or choke with sobs my hated breath.
The tremulous sob of the complaining owl.
